vulnerability.title: Configure Time Service Maxpoll Interval
vulnerability.description: The maxpoll should be configured to in /etc/ntp.conf or /etc/chrony.conf (or /etc/chrony.d/) to continuously poll time servers. To configure maxpoll in /etc/ntp.conf or /etc/chrony.conf (or /etc/chrony.d/) add the following after each server, pool or peer entry: maxpoll to server directives. If using chrony, any pool directives should be configured too.
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core && { ( rpm --quiet -q chrony || rpm --quiet -q ntp ); }; then var_time_service_set_maxpoll='' pof="/usr/sbin/pidof" CONFIG_FILES="/etc/ntp.conf" $pof ntpd || { CHRONY_D_PATH=/etc/chrony.d/ mapfile -t CONFIG_FILES < <(find ${CHRONY_D_PATH}.* -type f -name '*.conf') CONFIG_FILES+=(/etc/chrony.conf) } # get list of ntp files for config_file in "${CONFIG_FILES[@]}" ; do # Set maxpoll values to var_time_service_set_maxpoll sed -i "s/^\(\(server\|pool\|peer\).*maxpoll\) [0-9,-][0-9]*\(.*\)$/\1 $var_time_service_set_maxpoll \3/" "$config_file" done for config_file in "${CONFIG_FILES[@]}" ; do # Add maxpoll to server, pool or peer entries without maxpoll grep "^\(server\|pool\|peer\)" "$config_file" | grep -v maxpo

triage_decision.reason: The finding pertains to configuring the NTP/Chrony maxpoll interval, which is a non‑intrusive time‑synchronization hardening step. It modifies only configuration files and does not affect authentication, boot processes, or low‑level system components, making it amenable to automated remediation without risking lockout.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: Potential time‑sync disruption if maxpoll value is set incorrectly; Misconfiguration could lead to drift or loss of accurate time source

previous_attempts.scan_output: Single-rule scan failed for openscap_066 (rule=xccdf_org.ssgproject.content_rule_chronyd_or_ntpd_set_maxpoll). Could not verify.

review_feedback: Review+QA approved but the verification scan failed. Try a different approach.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: Remediation correctly adds maxpoll to NTP/Chrony config files and restarts the service, addressing the rule.
concerns: Uses regex to modify config files which may corrupt syntax if not carefully applied
Does not robustly verify existing maxpoll value before editing
Restarting the daemon may cause a brief loss of time sync
Script may affect both ntp and chrony simultaneously if both packages present without proper selection
suggested_improvements: Employ a proper INI parser or a safer configuration templating approach to insert maxpoll
Add checks to preserve existing maxpoll values and avoid duplicate entries
Include verification steps that validate syntax before service restart
Limit changes to the actively running daemon process
Provide configurable maxpoll value instead of hard‑coded default

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The script adds maxpoll directives but uses regex that may corrupt syntax and does not handle existing maxpoll values or differentiate between NTP and Chrony properly.
concerns: Regex-based modification can corrupt configuration syntax if lines contain comments or unexpected spacing
Does not check for existing maxpoll entries, risking duplicate directives
May apply changes to both chrony and ntp configurations simultaneously without clear distinction
Reloads the daemon after edit, which could cause a brief interruption in time sync
Hard-coded maxpoll value (6) may not meet policy requirements
Lacks robust validation of resulting configuration before restart
suggested_improvements: Use a dedicated configuration parser or line-in-file module to safely insert maxpoll only where missing
Detect the active daemon and limit changes to its primary configuration file
Check for existing maxpoll lines and preserve or replace them as needed
Validate the edited configuration with the daemon's syntax checker before reload
Make the maxpoll value configurable rather than hard-coded
Create backups and optionally require confirmation before applying changes
